# Growth in supermarket deliveries is decreasing

[Stand of Sectors](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/stand-van-sectoren/index.html)

[Retail](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/retail/index.html)

22/05/2025 • 4 minutes reading

[Ward van der Stee](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/ward-van-der-stee/index.html)

[Gerarda Westerhuis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/gerarda-westerhuis/index.html)

Fatima El Kharraz

[Henk Hofstede](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/henk-hofstede/index.html)

Show all authors

After years of strong growth in grocery deliveries by supermarkets, this now appears to be leveling off. ABN AMRO's aggregated and anonymized transaction data shows that the number of deliveries has increased by 'only' 11 percent in the past twelve months. In 2023 and for the whole of 2024, the average monthly growth was 69 and 21 percent respectively.

## Download the complete report

Read more about economic developments in the retail sector in the report 'Growth in supermarket delivery is declining'.

- [Stand of Retail (May 2025)

  575 KB](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/stand-van-retail-mei-2025_tcm16-264364.pdf)

Albert Heijn started delivering online orders in 2001. However, grocery delivery did not really get off the ground for a long time. The founding of Picnic in 2015, which only handles online orders and has no stores, accelerated the grocery delivery market. The corona pandemic further increased its popularity and caused a real growth explosion. Delivery growth has become less strong since the summer of 2023 and has declined slightly in recent months. According to research agency Circana, the online turnover share was [7.3 percent in 2024] (https://www.levenmiddelskrant.nl/circana--ah--picnic-en-dirk-winnen-meeste-marktaandeel) and 6.6 percent in 2023.

Despite the slowdown that followed the pandemic, growth in supermarket deliveries continues. In the past twelve months, the average monthly growth was 11 percent compared to the same period a year earlier. For the whole of 2024, the average growth was 21 percent. The number of online orders in April this year was more than three times as high as in 2019, while the number of transactions in the physical supermarket only increased by 21 percent.

##Dates and disclaimer

ABN AMRO's transaction data is aggregated based on private accounts and completely anonymized. The data says nothing about spending by non-residents and spending on business accounts. We use transaction data to gain insight into economic flows. The results of the research cannot be traced back to a natural person and are exclusively intended for research into economic trends.

## Competition between online and physical

The leveling off of growth in grocery delivery is caused by several factors. The high inflation in recent years makes prices feel high. As a result, consumers have started shopping more consciously and are paying more attention to price and additional delivery costs. In addition, geopolitical tensions, such as wars and trade wars, keep consumer confidence low, making consumers more cautious with their spending.

These problems also affect physical stores, which performed poorly last year. According to Statistics Netherlands (CBS), volumes fell by 3.9 percent and turnover by 1 percent at supermarkets. Given the continued growth of online orders, the decline can be entirely attributed to physical supermarkets. An important explanation for this, as mentioned earlier, is that consumers pay more attention to prices. In addition, the tobacco sales ban came into effect for all types of supermarkets halfway through last year. The ban has also caused a loss of additional sales.

The difficult year for physical supermarkets is only to a limited extent due to the rise of delivery. As indicated, competition for shopping in stores through grocery delivery has grown significantly less rapidly than in previous years.

The home delivery market appears to be slowly saturating and will become increasingly dependent on population growth and changes in consumer behavior. For example, the growing group of young people with little time, who use delivery services more often. However, all major supermarket chains in the Netherlands now offer delivery and they can deliver almost anywhere in the Netherlands. In addition, Crisp, for example, which also has no physical stores, chooses to focus on fresh food products, meal boxes and ready-made meals in order to distinguish itself from other online supermarkets.

The growth in the number of orders for the online supermarket will continue for a while, but it is expected that this will gradually become less strong. Supermarkets would do well to look at how they can further expand the market for online groceries and how they can further optimize their deliveries.

## More information

Read more about economic developments in the retail sector in the report 'Growth in supermarket delivery is declining'.

- [Stand of Retail (May 2025)

  575 KB](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/media/stand-van-retail-mei-2025_tcm16-264364.pdf)

## Read more in the retail sector

Today's store is more than just a pantry. It offers customers advice and inspiration and is the place to be in contact with customers. Due to the Ukraine crisis and significant inflation, consumer confidence has fallen sharply and this affects consumer behavior. Retailers are also faced with significant cost increases in the areas of purchasing, rent, personnel costs, Covid recovery payments and energy. Circumstances remain challenging for many retailers even after Covid.

[View all articles](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/retail/index.html)
